prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds exhibit mixed trend directions (two down, one up) with extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.50) and very high seasonality variance (0.91). CWT_catboost best tracks trends while ETS captures seasonality most effectively. Given the significant model diversity and complementary strengths across horizons, DBA combination is recommended to leverage all tier1 models' unique contributions rather than relying on a single champion.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ETS",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "ONLY_FT_rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "ETS", "FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "THETA", "ONLY_FT_rf", "CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["svr", "ONLY_FT_svr", "CWT_svr", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_svr", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.5004,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds exhibit mixed trend directions (two down, one up) with extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.50) and very high seasonality variance (0.91). CWT_catboost best tracks trends while ETS captures seasonality most effectively. Given the significant model diversity and complementary strengths across horizons, DBA combination is recommended to leverage all tier1 models' unique contributions rather than relying on a single champion."
}
raw.skeptic: {"add_names": [], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use validation-based fitting with expanding rolling window. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.00037) indicating competitive candidates. Universe leaderboards show robust_median (RMSE: 6796) but its universe score (0.71) is lower than current candidates (~0.84), so not adding. No redundancy issues - all 4 candidates serve different purposes.", "changes": [], "when_good": ""}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds exhibit mixed trend directions (two down, one up) with extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.50) and very high seasonality variance (0.91). CWT_catboost best tracks trends while ETS captures seasonality most effectively. Given the significant model diversity and complementary strengths across horizons, DBA combination is recommended to leverage all tier1 models' unique contributions rather than relying on a single champion.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity requiring selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.21 suggests moderate disagreement (below 0.25 threshold but still notable). RMSE spread_ratio=1.50 shows extreme model dispersion favoring top-k over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase shifts and leveraging complementary strengths across horizons. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 reduces instability risk while still capturing diverse signals.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all use validation-based fitting with expanding rolling window. Score margin between top2 is minimal (0.00037) indicating competitive candidates. Universe leaderboards show robust_median (RMSE: 6796) but its universe score (0.71) is lower than current candidates (~0.84), so not adding. No redundancy issues - all 4 candidates serve different purposes.
